lede: A look at January 8 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Elvis Presley and David Bowie, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Front for the Liberation of the Enclave of Cabinda
events.desc: Gunmen from an offshoot of the Front for the Liberation of the Enclave of Cabinda attack a bus carrying the Togo national football team on its way to the 2010 Africa Cup of Nations, killing three people and injuring another nine.

events.title: George W. Bush
events.desc: President of the United States George W. Bush signs into law the No Child Left Behind Act.

events.title: 1977 Moscow bombings
events.desc: Three bombs explode in Moscow, Russia, Soviet Union, within 37 minutes, killing seven. The bombings are attributed to an Armenian separatist group.

events.title: Charles de Gaulle
events.desc: Charles de Gaulle is proclaimed as the first President of the French Fifth Republic.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Britain introduces food rationing.

events.title: Woodrow Wilson
events.desc: U.S. President Woodrow Wilson announces his "Fourteen Points" as conditions for ending World War I.

events.title: Herman Hollerith
events.desc: Herman Hollerith is issued US patent #395,791 for the 'Art of Applying Statistics' — his punched card calculator.

events.title: George Washington
events.desc: George Washington delivers the first State of the Union address in New York City.

events.title: George Frideric Handel
events.desc: The premiere of George Frideric Handel's Ariodante takes place at the Royal Opera House, Covent Garden.

events.title: Emperor Huai of Jin
events.desc: Sima Chi becomes emperor of the Jin dynasty in succession to his brother, Sima Zhong, despite a challenge from his other brother, Sima Ying.

births.name: John Curtin
births.line: Australian journalist and politician, 14th Prime Minister of Australia

births.name: John Carroll (archbishop of Baltimore)
births.line: American archbishop, founder of Georgetown University

deaths.text: François Mitterrand, French sergeant and politician, 21st President of France
Robert Baden-Powell, 1st Baron Baden-Powell, English general and founder of the Scout movement
Galileo Galilei, Italian physicist, mathematician, astronomer, and philosopher
Giotto, Italian painter and architect, designed Scrovegni Chapel and Giotto's Campanile
